titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Cynthia Earl Kerman Papers Relating to The Lives of Jean Toomer
Kerman (Cynthia Earl) Papers Relating to The Lives of Jean Toomer
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday September 19, 2018 at 03:29
Transformed with yale.addEadidUrl.xsl. Adds @url with handle for finding aid. Overwrites @url if already present.

did.unittitle: Cynthia Earl Kerman Papers Relating to The Lives of Jean Toomer

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Research files, including notes, manuscripts, printed material, photographs, and audio tapes compiled by Cynthia Earl Kerman in the process of writing The Lives of Jean Toomer .

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Box 6 (audiocassettes): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Cynthia Earl Kerman Papers Relating to The Lives of Jean Toomer is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: The Cynthia Earl Kerman Papers Relating to the Lives of Jean Toomer . Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This collection received a basic level of processing in 2014, including rehousing and in some instances minimal organization.

The finding aid for this collection is compiled from individual preliminary lists for each acquisition that were created at or around the time of receipt by the library. The preliminary lists were migrated to comply with current archival descriptive standards and merged into a single file in 2007-2008. As part of the migration, modifications were made to the formatting of individual lists; however, the content of the lists was neither modified nor verified.

As a rule, descriptive information found in the Collection Contents section is drawn in large part from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.
This collection includes materials previously identified by the call number Uncat MSS 62.

bioghist.head: Cynthia Earl Kerman (1923-2012)
bioghist.p: Cynthia Earl Kerman, biographer of Jean Toomer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Research files, including notes, manuscripts, printed material, photographs, and audio tapes compiled by Cynthia Earl Kerman in the process of writing The Lives of Jean Toomer .

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two groupings: July 2000 Acquisition and January 2013 Acquisition.

persname.part: Kerman, Cynthia Earl. Lives of Jean Toomer.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into eight series: I. Biographical Research Files, 1923-1984. II. Notes and Copies of Writings by Toomer, 1949-1982. III. People Connected With Toomer, 1978-1987. IV. Interviews (Transcriptions), 1970-1983. V. Interviews (Audiocassettes), 1975-1983. VI. Chronological Notes, 1983-1995. VII. Unsorted Research Files, 1982-1983. VIII. Printed Material, 1966-1982.

did.unittitle: Notes and Copies of Writings by Toomer
Alphabetical list with dates and Kerman's notes
Books II and III, Earth-Being (from MCT collection)
From Exile Into Being, version I
From Exile Into Being, version II, part 1
From Exile Into Being, version II, part 2
Remember and return and first statements
Friends of Marjorie Content Toomer and Jean Toomer
Marjorie Content Toomer, references by others

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 6 (audiocassettes): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Marjorie Toomer / Elise Boulding / Larry Miller
Louise and William Welch (Tape III) / Elizabeth Delza Munson (Tape I)
Marjorie Toomer / Dorothy Paxson / Douglas and Dorothy Steere
Notes on Toomer's interpretation of 1926 experience
Gurdjieff: Views from the Real World
Hulme, Kathryn: Undiscovered Country. In Search of Gurdjieff
Nicoll, Maurice: Psychological Commentaries on the Teachings of Gurdjieff and Ouspensky
Transcription of interviews with Marjorie Toomer
